results.title: Native gas tokens can become stuck in ASDRouter contract
results.reasoning: Yes. Audit item #18 (“Excess msg.value from LayerZero Execution is Permanently Locked in Router”) identifies the same root cause: _sendASD does not refund leftover msg.value after a successful lzCompose execution, leaving native tokens stuck in ASDRouter. It cites the same function/code path and mechanism (using only _payload._feeForSend and not refunding the remainder). Although it explicitly highlights the cross-chain success branch, the underlying flaw and fix (refund remaining native balance to the refund address) apply equally to the local-transfer branch described by the vulnerability. Fixing the audit’s issue would also fix the reported stuck-native-tokens vulnerability.

results.title: Dual transaction nature of composed message transfer allows anyone to steal user funds
results.reasoning: Yes. Issue #1 in the audit explicitly identifies the missing msg.sender validation on ASDRouter.lzCompose and explains that, because LayerZero v2 executes lzCompose in a separate transaction after tokens are delivered, anyone can call lzCompose and steal the in-transit funds. It cites the same function, the same dual-transaction mechanism, and proposes the same type of fix (restrict sender to endpoint/whitelist). Fixing this reported issue would fix the described vulnerability.
